evidence: BlackLabSnippetParser marks 4 methods @Deprecated with replacement guidance, yet BlackLabQueryExecutor actively calls 3 of them: extractCollocateFromSnippet (lines 325 and 431), extractCollocateFromMatch (line 480), extractCollocateFromPlainText (line 483) — the deprecated API is the live code path

BlackLabSnippetParser's own 'clean API' section introduces extractCollocateLemma as a replacement for extractCollocateFromSnippet, but its body simply calls the deprecated method — the new API is a non-migrated wrapper

SemanticFieldExplorer class javadoc (lines 54-56) explicitly defers a model/ package refactor: 'These should be extracted to a dedicated model/ package in a future refactor (see issue 28bc8918 / a430f585)' — a tracked migration with no concrete completion criteria
WordSketchApiServer TODO(42e2a9a1): 'Once SEF inner classes are moved to a dedicated model/ package, update these imports' — two linked files with an open migration dependency between them

WordSketchApiServer TODO(595bb84c): 'Move HttpServer creation and route registration here so start() only calls server.start()' — a structural refactor deferred with a note that it requires a breaking API change (IOException on constructor)

strengths: Deprecated methods in BlackLabSnippetParser are explicitly annotated with @Deprecated and their Javadoc points to named replacements, making the intended migration direction clear
TODO comments reference specific issue hashes, providing traceability rather than anonymous stale notes
incomplete_migration.issue_character: The migration debt has two tiers: an API-rename migration in BlackLabSnippetParser where new names were added but callers were never updated (deprecated-but-actively-called), and a structural refactor in the query/api boundary where inner DTOs and constructor responsibilities are acknowledged as misplaced but deferred.
incomplete_migration.score_rationale: The codebase has a clear migration intent documented in comments and @Deprecated annotations, which is better than silent debt. However, both migration tracks are in a state where the old and new patterns coexist without a timeline or gating condition: 3 deprecated methods are the live call path, the 'new' extractCollocateLemma still calls the deprecated original, and two linked TODOs describe a structural refactor with no progress. A developer adding a new extraction method or a new SEF result type must choose which pattern to follow and cannot find a definitive answer in the code. This places the score in the 65-70 range — well below solid (80) but above pervasive drift (60).

issues.summary: 3 @Deprecated BlackLabSnippetParser methods remain the active call path in BlackLabQueryExecutor
evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor line 325: extractCollocateFromSnippet(matchXml) — the @Deprecated method is called inside extractCollocateLemma helper
BlackLabQueryExecutor line 480: extractCollocateFromMatch(identity, collocateLabelPos) in executeSurfacePattern
BlackLabQueryExecutor line 483: extractCollocateFromPlainText(identity, collocateLabelPos) in executeSurfacePattern
issues.suggestion: Replace each call site with the declared replacement: extractCollocateFromSnippet → extractCollocateLemma(xml) or extractLemmaAt(xml, n); extractCollocateFromMatch → extractLemmaAt(matchOnly, labelPos); extractCollocateFromPlainText → extractCollocateFromMatchText(text, position). Once no callers remain, delete the deprecated methods.

evidence: extractCollocateLemma(matchXml) body: return extractCollocateFromSnippet(matchXml) — the replacement wraps the deprecated method without migrating the implementation
Callers of the 'new' API trigger the deprecated implementation on every call
issues.suggestion: Move the LEMMA_ATTR regex loop from extractCollocateFromSnippet directly into extractCollocateLemma, then update extractCollocateFromSnippet to delegate to extractCollocateLemma (or delete it). This inverts the delegation direction so the new name owns the implementation.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer inner DTOs are documented as needing a model/ package move but have no completion criteria
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer javadoc lines 54-56: 'These should be extracted to a dedicated model/ package in a future refactor (see issue 28bc8918 / a430f585)'
WordSketchApiServer TODO(42e2a9a1) depends on that migration completing before its own imports can be cleaned up — two files in a holding pattern
issues.suggestion: Create pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.model package and move ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, Edge, ExploreOptions into it. Update imports in both SemanticFieldExplorer and WordSketchApiServer. Remove both TODO comments once done.

root_causes: The BlackLabSnippetParser 'clean API' section was added in a naming refactor pass that introduced replacements but did not update callers or invert the implementation ownership
The SEF model/ migration was scoped as a future task with issue references but no gating condition, leaving two files in a mutual wait state
